sources.issuing_organisation: British Society for Allergy and Clinical Immunology
sources.exact_document_title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
sources.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 9.1–9.8 — allergy testing and investigation context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or diagnosed asthma, excluding severe asthma and acute attacks.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist diagnosis, monitoring, and chronic asthma management.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for chronic asthma assessment and monitoring; excludes severe asthma and acute attacks and is not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.4 — monitoring asthma control and uncontrolled asthma
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1 and 1.14.1–1.14.2 — inhaler technique, adherence, and action plans
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.
